articles.title: Jaishankar in Bulgaria, discusses bilateral ties, India-EU partnership
articles.description: EAM S Jaishankar visited Bulgaria for talks on bilateral cooperation and India-EU ties. He noted India's 7-8% growth, offering new opportunities in engineering and defence, and invited Bulgaria to deepen connections in all sectors.

articles.title: Democracy 'brutally killed': Cong slams BJP over Natarajan's RS nom
articles.description: Congress leader Sukhdeo Bhagat attacked the BJP over the rejection of Meenakshi Natarajan's Rajya Sabha nomination from MP, calling it a "brutal killing" of democracy. Natarajan has now challenged the Returning Officer's decision in the Supreme Court.

articles.title: Virat Kohli refuses to pose as daughter Vamika waits for him: ‘Respect his privacy’
articles.description: In a recent video on Instagram, cricketer Virat Kohli refused to pose for paparazzi at the airport, because he wanted to meet his daughter Vamika.

articles.title: African proverb of the day: ''Now the marriage begins, ' says the woman who has been..." - Why real test begins after the honeymoon phase gets over
articles.description: This Swahili proverb reveals that true commitment is tested through life's hardships, showing how resilience and understanding strengthen relationships.

articles.title: Kharge jealous of PM Modi's longest tenure, claims Telangana BJP
articles.description: Telangana BJP spokesperson Prakash Reddy has slammed Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge, claiming he is 'jealous' of PM Modi's longest continuous tenure. Kharge had earlier accused the BJP of 'twisting history' to create a new category for Modi.

articles.title: PM Modi's Kisan-first approach hailed as foundation of Viksit Bharat
articles.description: Union Ministers Nitin Gadkari and Piyush Goyal hailed PM Modi's 'Kisan-first approach' on the NDA government's 12th anniversary. They praised farmer welfare schemes like e-NAM, PMFBY, and PM-KISAN as the foundation of Viksit Bharat 2047.

articles.title: Modi's 12-year tenure will be written in golden letters: Kiren Rijiju
articles.description: Union Minister Kiren Rijiju hailed PM Modi's 12-year tenure, stating the period's parliamentary reforms and landmark legislations will be 'written in golden letters'. He cited these achievements as historic steps towards building a 'Viksit Bharat'.

articles.title: Constitution in danger: Patwari on Natarajan's RS nomination row
articles.description: MP Congress President Jitu Patwari slammed the rejection of Meenakshi Natarajan's Rajya Sabha nomination, alleging political pressure. The party has moved the Supreme Court, while a legal expert has dismissed the move as a 'political stunt'.

articles.title: Dialogue, diplomacy only way forward, not an era of war: Jaishankar
articles.description: EAM S Jaishankar, in Bulgaria, reiterated India's stance that dialogue and diplomacy are the way forward in an era of conflict. He highlighted the need for supply chain resilience and unimpeded maritime trade, speaking for the Global South.

articles.title: Chirag Paswan, Samrat Choudhary wish Lalu Prasad Yadav on his birthday
articles.description: Union Minister Chirag Paswan and Bihar Deputy CM Samrat Choudhary were among leaders across party lines who wished RJD chief Lalu Prasad Yadav on his 78th birthday, praying for his long life and good health.

articles.title: NITI Aayog meeting: CMs Revanth Reddy, Naidu, Vijay arrive in Delhi
articles.description: CMs Revanth Reddy, Chandrababu Naidu and Vijay have arrived in Delhi for the 11th NITI Aayog meeting, chaired by PM Modi. Vijay also met President Murmu, Rahul Gandhi and other leaders. The meeting's theme is 'Inclusive Human Development'.

articles.title: PM Modi's tenure: Dilip Ghosh lauds PM, says Congress 'troubled'
articles.description: West Bengal Minister Dilip Ghosh lauded PM Modi's tenure, stating people are happy while the Congress is 'troubled'. The remark came after Congress's Mallikarjun Kharge criticised BJP's claims, accusing them of distorting history.

articles.title: Even PM Modi's Family Has Six Siblings: Owaisi Questions SIR Form Cap On Listing Children
articles.description: Even PM Modi's Family Has Six Siblings: Owaisi Questions SIR Form Cap On Listing Children

articles.title: Watch: US rains over 39 Tomahawk missiles in fiery 'self-defence' strikes on Iran
articles.description: The US Central Command released video of self-defense strikes against Iranian military targets, including radar and air defense sites. The operation, involving 39 Tomahawk missiles, was a response to Iran's alleged aggression threatening US forces and international shipping. This marks a significant escalation in regional tensions.

articles.title: US Israel Iran War News Live Updates: Iran claims missile strike targeted US F-35, F-16 jets at Jordan base
articles.description: US-Israel-Iran War News Today Live Updates: Iran’s IRGC claimed it launched a missile strike on a US-linked command centre in Jordan, targeting Al-Azraq Air Base with 12 ballistic missiles.

articles.title: Any Vessel Approaching Hormuz Strait Will Be Considered 'Collaborating With Enemy' - IRGC
articles.description: Iran will consider any vessel approaching the Strait of Hormuz as "collaborating with the enemy," the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy said in a statement.

articles.title: The Modi era: Reimagining governance for a new India
articles.description: History seldom moves in a straight line. Every generation confronts its own challenges and must find answers suited to its time. India’s journey from Independence to the present has been shaped by leaders who responded...

articles.title: Iranian Envoy Says US Strikes Prove Military Force Cannot Deliver Lasting Security Or Stability
articles.description: Iran's Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Amir Saeid Iravani, told the Security Council that lasting peace and stability in the Middle East require addressing the root causes of regional conflicts. He pointed to territorial occupations, Israeli military actions and the prolonged US military presence as key drivers of instability. Iravani also argued that recent US strikes against Iran showed foreign military deployments cannot guarantee security. The Iranian envoy urged Washington to abandon threats and intimidation and pursue diplomacy based on mutual respect, sovereign equality and international law.#Iran #UnitedNations #UNSC #MiddleEast #IranUN #AmirSaeidIravani #USIran #IsraelIran #Diplomacy #WorldNews #BreakingNews #Geopolitics #SecurityCouncil #MiddleEastConflict #InternationalRelations

articles.title: Trump Weighs Short, Large-Scale Military Operation Against Iran At White House Meeting: Report
articles.description: The proposal was reportedly discussed as Trump met with senior national security officials amid a rapidly deteriorating security situation in West Asia.

articles.title: 'Heroes of America, ICE': Trump signs $70B bill to supercharge deportations and border enforcement
articles.description: President Donald Trump signed a sweeping $70 billion immigration enforcement package, delivering a major victory for his administration's border security agenda and providing long-term funding for ICE and Border Patrol operations.

articles.description: After 40 years of covering American politics, I've seen presidents spin, deflect and dodge with remarkable skill. But what unfolded during President Trump's "Meet the Press" interview with Kristen Welker on Sunday wasn't spin. It was something far more troubling: a president who seems genuinely convinced that reality bends to whatever ...

articles.title: Maine Democrats pick toxic waste dump Platner
articles.description: To no one's great surprise, Maine Democrats chose Graham Platner on Tuesday as their candidate for the United States Senate.

articles.title: Shatrughan Sinha, Saayoni Ghosh - Surprise names in 19 TMC rebels list
articles.description: 19 Trinamool Congress (TMC) MPs have now reportedly signed a list of rebels. These include Saayoni Ghosh, Shatrughan Sinha, and Yusuf Pathan. Meanwhile, a parallel rebellion has also shaken the West Bengal Assembly, led by Ritabrata Banerjee.

articles.title: Govt. to have a relook on media curbs at Secretariat
articles.description: Amid accusations of restrictions imposed for mediapersons at the Secretariat, Chief Minister V.D.Satheesan said on Wednesday that the State government will need to take a relook at a 2018 circular during the tenure of the previous government which had led to the restrictions.

articles.title: Upasana Singh hails PM Modi, says his leadership inspired her to vote
articles.description: Actor Upasana Singh praised PM Narendra Modi, revealing his leadership inspired her to vote for the first time after being disillusioned with politics. She believes India has benefited greatly under his tenure and feels safer due to his leadership.

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee Faces Existential Crisis As TMC Rebels Challenge Leadership | Brass Tacks | News18
articles.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee is facing one of the most significant political challenges of her career as internal dissent within the Trinamool Congress continues to intensify. A growing rebel faction has raised questions about the party's unity, leadership structure, and future direction, fueling speculation about possible political realignments. Amid the turmoil, discussions surrounding the TMC's long-term strategy, including reports of closer coordination with the Congress, have added another layer of uncertainty. n18oc_brass-tacks News18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube

articles.title: 'My Supreme Privilege': PM Modi On Becoming India's Longest Serving Prime Minister
articles.description: Recalling his political journey, PM Modi said he had continued to move forward despite facing numerous challenges and setbacks.

articles.title: PoK Revolt Gives India Ammunition To Expose Pakistan's Lies; Will Delhi Grab Opportunity?
articles.description: Pakistan has spent decades painting a pretty picture of Kashmir that does not want to be with India. For long, Islamabad has been engaged in narrative war over with Delhi on Kashmir. But events unfolding in Pakistan-Occupied Kashmir are raising uncomfortable questions for Islamabad. With PoK on fire, the question isn't whether Pakistan's story is falling apart. The real question is whether India is ready, willing and equipped to finally tell its own story to a world that hasn't always been listening.

articles.title: "Office Politics Won": Man Quits 18-Year Career With 6 Months Of Savings
articles.description: After years of dedication, he felt that promotions and recognition often went to others, leaving him frustrated by workplace politics.

articles.title: City Commissioner of Police flags off Singappen Special Force vehicles in Madurai
articles.description: Madurai's City Police Commissioner launches Singappen Special Force to enhance safety for women and children with dedicated teams.

articles.title: Congress out, new camp in? Ritabrata Banerjee hints at rebels’ next move as TMC crisis deepens
articles.description: Ritabrata Banerjee rejects Congress merger rumours, claims support of 64 MLAs and signals an independent future for the TMC rebel faction amid Bengal's political turmoil.

articles.title: Mutalik warns writer Bhagawan for ‘statement’ undermining Lord Rama
articles.description: Pramod Mutalik warns writer K.S. Bhagawan to cease statements undermining Lord Rama and Hindu beliefs, citing constitutional concerns.

articles.title: IRGC Rains Fire On US Ally; F-35 Jet Hangars Attacked; 'Crushing & Decisive Operation’
articles.description: Iran claims it has launched coordinated attacks against multiple U.S. military targets across the region. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) says it targeted the U.S. al-Azraq Air Base in Jordan, the Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait, and the headquarters of the U.S. Fifth Fleet in Bahrain in response to recent American strikes. The attacks come after President Donald Trump accused Iran of bringing down a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, a claim strongly denied by Tehran. Jordan says it intercepted missiles aimed at al-Azraq, while U.S. officials say the vast majority of Iranian missiles and drones were shot down before reaching their targets.#iran #usa #usiran #iranvsus #middleeast #irgc #donaldtrump #jordan #kuwait #bahrain #fifthfleet #straitofhormuz #usmilitary #missileattack #droneattack #middleeastcrisis #irannews #usnews

articles.title: Israeli PM Netanyahu to run for re-election after Trump raises doubts
articles.description: Benjamin Netanyahu will seek re-election this year, his party announced on Wednesday, after U.S. President Donald Trump said he wasn't sure if the Israeli prime minister would stand again.

articles.title: Starmer condemns Belfast anti-immigrant violence after knife attack; says ‘those responsible will feel full force of law’
articles.description: Starmer condemns Belfast anti-immigrant violence after knife attack; says ‘those responsible will feel full force of law’

articles.title: ‘Can’t be mute spectator’: Supreme Court asks Kerala government to take charge of elephant in ownership row
articles.description: The Supreme Court noted that it was ‘truly unfortunate’ that the elephant Uttoly Raman was ‘subjected to commercial exploitation’ despite a court order.

articles.title: Calcutta High Court grants interim protection to ex-minister Aroop Biswas in Messi event case
articles.description: The case file was reopened after the BJP took office in West Bengal, based on the complaint of Satadru Dutta, the organiser of Messi’s event at Salt Lake Stadium.

articles.title: Sushmita Dev Quits TMC & Rajya Sabha, Delivering Fresh Blow To Mamata Banerjee As TMC Crisis Deepens
articles.description: Senior Trinamool Congress leader Sushmita Dev has resigned from the party & Rajya Sabha, marking the second resignation by a TMC MP in recent days after Sukhendu Sekhar Roy's exit. The development comes amid growing turmoil within Mamata Banerjee's party following its defeat in the West Bengal Assembly elections. Dev's recent meeting with Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has further fuelled speculation about her political future, even as the party grapples with rebellion from MLAs, dissent among MPs and a widening leadership crisis.

articles.title: Why a city in Lebanon could decide whether the Iran-Israel war ends or starts again
articles.description: Iran says any ceasefire must include Lebanon. Israel says Lebanon is a separate battlefield. That disagreement is becoming the biggest threat to fragile efforts to prevent a wider regional war.

articles.title: TMC MP Sushmita Dev's resignation accepted by Rajya Sabha Chairman C P Radhakrishnan from Upper House, TMC faces another setback
RS Chairman C P Radhakrishnan accepts TMC MP Sushmita Dev's resignation
articles.description: Rajya Sabha Chairman C P Radhakrishnan accepted TMC MP Sushmita Dev's resignation. This marks another setback for Mamata Banerjee's TMC. Dev cited a long story for her decision. Her exit follows Sukhendu Sekhar Ray's resignation. The party faces internal turmoil and speculation of a split. Dev joined TMC in 2021 after leaving Congress.
